retry_reason: No failure possible — refcount operations always succeed; the condition only decides whether to update, not whether the update succeeds.
contract_summary: This function manages a single slot (*keep) to hold a reference to the latest fence among a sequence of fence objects. It compares the existing fence in *keep with the new fence parameter; if the new fence is earlier or equal, it does nothing. Otherwise, it releases the old reference via dma_fence_put and stores a newly acquired reference (via dma_fence_get) to the new fence in *keep. After the call, *keep always holds a valid reference. The caller must eventually release the final reference stored in *keep. No return value to check.

judge.reason: Function's sole purpose is to keep a reference to the later fence by conditionally releasing the old and acquiring a new reference; the comparison is trivial and directly supports the refcount decision.

check.reason: Both operations are directly invoked in the code: dma_fence_put(*keep) and dma_fence_get(fence). The access paths correctly trace from the function interface (keep[0] for the old fence object, fence for the new fence object) to the refcount field via the type chain dma_fence--kref--refcount_t. Segment counts match. No inconsistencies found.
